geographicSummary: District Seven is the central heart of Columbus, encompassing Downtown, German Village, Merion Village, the Brewery District, portions of Italian Village, and Franklinton. The district extends south toward I-270 and Grove City. Its racial diversity and income levels closely mirror the city average.

pointsOfInterest.name: The Book Loft of German Village
pointsOfInterest.description: 32-room independent bookstore housed in pre-Civil War buildings with over 500,000 books in stock.

pointsOfInterest.name: Schiller Park
pointsOfInterest.description: 23.6-acre park in German Village with gazebo, gardens, fishing pond, and free summer Shakespeare performances.

pointsOfInterest.name: Scioto Audubon Metro Park
pointsOfInterest.description: 119-acre park along the Scioto River with nature center, climbing wall, and birding trails.

pointsOfInterest.name: German Village Historic District
pointsOfInterest.description: Nationally recognized historic neighborhood with 19th-century brick architecture, shops, and restaurants.

pointsOfInterest.name: COSI (Center of Science and Industry)
pointsOfInterest.description: Major science museum on the Scioto Mile with interactive exhibits, planetarium, and outdoor learning spaces.

pointsOfInterest.name: Columbus Commons
pointsOfInterest.description: Downtown urban park built on the former City Center Mall site with gardens, playground, and seasonal events.

areaCommissions.name: Franklinton Area Commission
areaCommissions.meetingSchedule: Second Tuesday of each month, 6:00 PM
areaCommissions.meetingLocation: Healthy Living Center, 777 W. State St.

areaCommissions.name: South Side Area Commission
areaCommissions.meetingSchedule: Third Tuesday of each month, 6:30 PM
areaCommissions.meetingLocation: Columbus Metropolitan Library — Parsons Branch, 1113 Parsons Ave.

data.data_note: Figures from original site launch. Block-group weighted aggregation to council districts.

snapshots.source: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 5-Year via Census API (build_district_profiles.py)

data.data_note: Median income, rent, and home value are population-weighted averages of block-group medians — approximations, not true district medians. See /data/ for methodology.
